probabilityAnalysis.title: Probability Analysis
probabilityAnalysis.subtitle: Method validation and recovery opportunities
probabilityAnalysis.powLearnTitle: Learn what PoW means and about the probability methods
probabilityAnalysis.powWhatIsDesc: PoW = Probability of Worthless — The probability that an option will expire worthless.

sections.calibrationCardDesc: Validates which probability calculation methods are most accurate by comparing predictions against actual market outcomes.
sections.recoveryCardDesc: Identifies recovery opportunities where options previously had high probability levels but have since declined—these statistically expire worthless more often than expected.
sections.stockPerformanceSubtitle: Compare Method Accuracy Across All Stocks
sections.recoveryAnalysisSubtitle: Identifying Systematic Mispricing Where Options Expire Worthless More Often Than Expected

probabilityAnalysis.howToReadCalibration1: The chart compares predicted probabilities (horizontal axis) against actual outcomes (vertical axis). The diagonal line represents perfect calibration. Methods above the line are conservative (safer than predicted); methods below are overconfident (riskier than predicted). Colored lines with dots represent different probability calculation methods—larger dots indicate more data points.
probabilityAnalysis.howToReadCalibration2: Use the dropdown menu to filter by individual stocks and see which probability methods are most reliable for your trading.
probabilityAnalysis.howToReadStockPerf1: This analysis compares how each probability method performs across different stocks. Each cell shows the average calibration error—how much the method's predictions deviate from actual outcomes. Positive values (green) indicate the method is conservative, predicting lower probabilities than actual results. Negative values (red) indicate overconfidence, predicting higher probabilities than actual results.
probabilityAnalysis.howToReadStockPerf2: Use the DTE selector to see how method performance changes as options approach expiration. Sort by any column to identify which methods work best for specific stocks.
probabilityAnalysis.finding: Validated Finding: Recovery Candidates Expire Worthless 1.17x More Often
probabilityAnalysis.statsFootnote: Based on 1,16M expired options analyzed across all probability methods and thresholds
probabilityAnalysis.recoveryWhatDesc: Recovery candidates are options that previously peaked at high probability (80%+, 85%+, 90%+, or 95%+) but have since declined to lower probability levels. Analysis shows these options expire worthless significantly more often than the probability model predicts—revealing systematic mispricing.
probabilityAnalysis.recoveryWhyMatters: The probability model underestimates their true worthless rates by {{adv1}} on average, while it underestimates for all options by only {{adv2}}. This {{gap}} gap proves recovery candidates are systematically mispriced, not just statistical noise.
probabilityAnalysis.howToReadRecovery: The chart compares worthless rates (green bars = recovery candidates vs red bars = all options) across different days-to-expiry periods. Higher green bars indicate better opportunities for put sellers.
probabilityAnalysis.bestOpportunitiesDTE: DTE: 36+ days show {{adv1}} advantage (vs {{adv2}} for 0-7 days)
probabilityAnalysis.bestOpportunitiesMethod: Method: Weighted Average shows {{adv1}} advantage (best for identifying recovery opportunities)
probabilityAnalysis.bestOpportunitiesFilter: Use the filters to explore which probability thresholds, methods, and current probability ranges show the strongest recovery advantages on specific stocks.

calibrationChart.title: Calibration Analysis
calibrationChart.noData: No calibration data available for this filter combination.
calibrationChart.interpretation: Lines closer to the diagonal indicate better calibration. Each dot represents a probability bin showing predicted vs actual rates.

methodComparisonChart.title: Stock Performance by Method
methodComparisonChart.colorGuide: Color Guide: Green = conservative (under-predicts), Red = overconfident (over-predicts), White = well-calibrated
methodComparisonChart.colorDesc: Values show average calibration error (actual − predicted) weighted by sample size. Positive = conservative, Negative = overconfident.
methodComparisonChart.noData: No data available for this filter combination.
methodComparisonChart.interpretation: Positive values indicate the method is conservative (predicts lower probabilities than actual), negative values indicate overconfidence. Values are weighted by sample size to emphasize results with more data.

ivAnalysis.title: Implied Volatility History
ivAnalysis.dataSourceNote: Each day's IV is a constant-maturity 30-day implied volatility computed via variance interpolation across the options term structure, targeting ~21 business days (following the Swedish holiday calendar). Dates with no valid IV data show as "–".
ivAnalysis.tooltip52w: Where today's IV sits within this stock's own 52-week range. 100 = IV at 52-week high (expensive options), 0 = IV at 52-week low (cheap options). Useful for comparing relative IV richness across stocks with different baseline volatility.
ivAnalysis.tooltipHistorical: Where today's IV sits within this stock's full historical range in the dataset. 100 = IV at all-time high, 0 = IV at all-time low.

supportLevelOptions.title: Support Level Options List
supportLevelOptions.subtitle: Filter put options by support levels and analyze positioning relative to support
supportLevelOptions.minDaysSinceBreak: Min Days Since Last Break (business days)
supportLevelOptions.noOptionsMatch: No options match the current criteria. Try relaxing some filters.
supportLevelOptions.tooltipStability: Percentage of trading days within the rolling period where the rolling low held without being broken. Higher percentage indicates more stable support. For example, 85% means the support level held on 85% of days and was broken on 15% of days.
supportLevelOptions.tooltipStrengthDesc: Composite score combining: Stability (30%), Days Since Break (25%), Break Frequency (25%), Drop Consistency (20%)
supportLevelOptions.tooltipTrendDesc: Compares first half vs second half of rolling period.
supportLevelOptions.tooltipMaxConsecDesc: Historical maximum number of consecutive support breaks in any cluster (breaks ≤30 days apart).
supportLevelOptions.tooltipMaxConsecScale: 0-2: Low cascade risk | 3-5: Normal | 6-10: Elevated | 11+: High risk
supportLevelOptions.tooltipCurrConsecDesc: Number of breaks in most recent active cluster (if ended ≤30 days ago).
supportLevelOptions.tooltipCurrConsecNote: If Current ≥ 80% of Max: likely rebound candidate!
supportLevelOptions.tooltipMaxConsecWorstCase: Shows worst-case scenario if stock starts breaking support.
supportLevelOptions.tooltipCurrConsec0: Support holding normally - safe to write

metrics.patternDesc: Pattern is assigned based on first matching rule (evaluated in order):
metrics.noBreaksRecent: No breaks in recent cluster (last cluster ended >30 days ago)
metrics.activeCluster: Active cluster with {{current}} breaks ({{pct}}% of max)
metrics.worstCase: Worst-case scenario: {{max}} consecutive breaks in one cluster
metrics.exhaustedDesc: Current breaks ({{current}}) ≥ 80% of historical max ({{max}}). This suggests the cascade may be near exhaustion - potential rebound candidate.
metrics.trendDescImproving: Second half stability exceeds first half by >5%
metrics.trendDescWeakening: Second half stability is >5% lower than first half
metrics.trendDescStable: Second half stability within ±5% of first half

recommendations.title: Automated Put Option Recommendations
recommendations.subtitle: Evaluates 6 weighted analysis factors—support strength, support stability, recovery potential, historical peaks, monthly seasonality, and current performance—to identify optimal put writing opportunities.
recommendations.weightsHint: Adjust sliders to set relative importance between factors.
recommendations.weightDaysSinceBreakDesc: Time since support was last broken
recommendations.weightHistoricalPeakDesc: Recovery candidate from high past probability
recommendations.filterMinDaysSinceBreak: Min Days Since Last Break (business days)
recommendations.noRecommendationsFound: No recommendations found. Try adjusting your filters or click Analyze.

explanation.strengthExplStrong: historically held reliably with few breaks
explanation.strengthExplModerate: held reasonably well with occasional breaks
explanation.intro: This analysis explains why {{optionName}} ({{stockName}}) has been recommended based on multiple factors.
explanation.supportText: The option has a strike price of {{strikePrice}} kr, which is {{strikeDir}} the {{period}} rolling low support level of {{rollingLow}} kr. The current price ({{currentPrice}} kr) is {{distancePct}}% {{priceDir}} the rolling low. This {{period}} low can act as a strong support level for the stock.
explanation.robustnessText: The support level has not been broken for {{days}} days, which is {{aboveBelow}} the minimum threshold of {{threshold}} days. The support strength score is {{score}}/100, indicating {{strength}} support robustness—this means the support level has {{strengthExpl}}.
explanation.robustnessExtended: The extended period since the last break makes this support level more reliable.
explanation.recoveryText: This option previously reached a {{peakPct}}% probability peak but has declined to {{currentPct}}%—qualifying as a "recovery candidate." Historical research shows that similar options ({{currentProbBin}} probability with {{dteBin}} days to expiry that previously peaked above {{thresholdPct}}%) expired worthless {{recoveryRatePct}}% of the time. This is substantially higher than the current {{currentPct}}% probability, indicating the market systematically underestimates these opportunities. For put sellers, this {{advantage}} percentage point advantage represents a statistical edge. See the Probability Analysis page → "Probability Recovery Analysis" for detailed research.
explanation.recoverySimpleText: This option previously reached a {{peakPct}}% probability peak but has declined to {{currentPct}}%, qualifying as a "recovery candidate." Research shows such options typically expire worthless more often than their current probability suggests, though specific historical data for this probability/DTE combination is limited.
explanation.probHistoryAbove: The {{methodName}} probability previously peaked at {{peakPct}}%, which is above the {{thresholdPct}}% threshold. The current probability is {{currentPct}}%.
explanation.probHistoryBelow: The {{methodName}} probability previously peaked at {{peakPct}}%, which is below the {{thresholdPct}}% threshold, so this is not classified as a recovery candidate. The current probability is {{currentPct}}%.
explanation.probAnalysisText: The current probability of {{currentPct}}% is very high (≥90%). Historical recovery data is not available for this probability bin, limiting comparative analysis.
explanation.seasonalityBase: Historical data shows that {{stockName}} has had positive performance during {{monthlyPct}}% of all {{month}} months in the dataset ({{monthsCount}} months of historical data).
explanation.seasonalityBaseMultiple: Historical data shows that {{stockName}} has had positive performance during {{monthlyPct}}% of all {{month}} months in the dataset (multiple months of historical data).
explanation.seasonalityLowDayClose: The stock typically hits monthly lows on day {{lowDay}} (which is very close to today (day {{today}}), suggesting we may be near a monthly low point).
explanation.seasonalityLowDayFar: The stock typically hits monthly lows on day {{lowDay}} (while today is day {{today}}).
explanation.perfBase: {{stockName}}'s current performance for {{month}} is {{currentPerf}}%, while the historical average for this month is {{avgReturn}}%.
explanation.perfUnder: The stock is currently underperforming by {{underperf}} percentage points, which could suggest potential for a bounce back.
explanation.perfOver: The stock is currently outperforming the historical average by {{overperf}} percentage points.
explanation.perfInLine: The stock is performing in line with historical averages.
explanation.perfWorstDrawdown: Historically, the worst intra-month drawdown for {{month}} has been {{drawdown}}%, providing context for potential downside risk.
explanation.compositeBase: Based on all these factors combined with customized weights, this option received a composite score of {{score}}/100, indicating a {{strength}} recommendation.
explanation.compositeHigh: This high score suggests multiple favorable conditions align for this option.
explanation.compositeModerate: This moderate score suggests some favorable conditions, but not all factors are optimal.
explanation.compositeLow: This lower score suggests caution - review individual factors to understand potential concerns.
explanation.finalBase: {{stockName}} option {{optionName}} with strike {{strikePrice}} kr and expiry {{expiryDate}} ({{daysToExpiry}} days) offers a premium of {{premium}} kr.
explanation.finalHigh: The combination of support level stability, probability recovery potential, and favorable seasonality makes this a compelling candidate for put option writing.
explanation.finalModerate: This option shows some promise based on the analysis, though individual factors should be carefully reviewed.
explanation.finalLow: Consider reviewing alternative options with higher composite scores, or adjust analysis weights to match your risk tolerance.

scoreBreakdown.title: Score Breakdown
scoreBreakdown.noDataWarning: No data available for this stock. Score contribution: 0 points (not included)
scoreBreakdown.howScoringDesc: Each factor is normalized to a 0-100 scale, then weighted according to its importance. Only factors with available data contribute to the final score.
scoreBreakdown.missingDataDesc: If a factor has no historical data for this stock, it contributes 0 points to the composite score, regardless of its weight setting.
scoreBreakdown.noDataBadgeDesc: This stock lacks the historical data needed for this factor, so it contributes 0 points.

supportStrength.label: Support reliability score
supportStrength.explanation: Composite metric (0-100) measuring how consistently support level has held based on stability, break frequency, and drop consistency

daysSinceBreak.label: Days since support break
daysSinceBreak.explanation: Number of trading days since support level was last tested/broken; higher is better

recoveryAdvantage.label: Recovery candidate rate
recoveryAdvantage.explanation: Percentage of similar options (by probability/DTE) that recovered from similar levels in historical data

historicalPeak.label: Historical peak probability
historicalPeak.explanation: Highest probability of worthlessness ever seen for this option; indicates recovery potential if peaked above current threshold

monthlySeasonality.label: Monthly positive rate
monthlySeasonality.explanation: Percentage of historical months with positive returns for this stock in the current calendar month

currentPerformance.label: Month-to-date underperformance
currentPerformance.explanation: How much the stock is underperforming (negative = better for puts) compared to its historical average for this calendar month

factorDescs.recoveryAdvantage: Historical worthless rate for similar recovery candidates
factorDescs.daysSinceBreak: Time since support level was last broken
factorDescs.historicalPeak: Recovery candidate based on historical probability peaks
factorDescs.monthlySeasonality: Historical positive return rate for current month
factorDescs.currentPerformance: Current month underperformance vs historical average

scoredOptions.toastFiltersResetDesc: Filters have been reset to default settings

calibration.title: Model Calibration & Accuracy
calibration.subtitle: When models predict 70-80% probability: Probability Optimization achieves 83.8% accuracy, TA ML Model 70.06%. TA ML Model validated on 7.4M+ out-of-sample records (May 2026 retrain).
calibration.v21Desc1: The Probability Optimization Model combines three factors to estimate the likelihood an option will expire worthless (out-of-the-money). This table shows the historical accuracy of predictions at different probability levels, based on analysis of 72,469 options with known outcomes.
calibration.v21Testing: In-sample calibration on the Probability Tracking System (options actively tracked with complete historical snapshots). The 83.8% accuracy at 70-80% represents how well the formula works on this specific tracked dataset. Sample size of 19,830 at 70-80% is sufficient for high statistical confidence on tracked options.
calibration.v21DataRequirement: Probability Optimization Model requires 5 complex probability calculation methods (Black-Scholes + calibration + historical volatility + Bayesian + ensemble). This requires months of special historical probability tracking data. Of 1.86M historical options, only 72,469 (3.9%) have complete: probability calculations + historical peak data + support metrics + known outcomes. This is why sample size is limited.
calibration.whyDifferentSizesDesc: Probability Optimization Model tests on 72,469 tracked options (Probability Tracking System) → 19,830 at 70-80%. TA Model (May 2026 retrain) validates on 7,375,016 out-of-sample option-day records (daily snapshots) → 1,617,173 at 70-80%. The large difference reflects different data sources: Probability Optimization is limited to tracked options; TA Model uses daily market data covering all Swedish equity options.
calibration.v21TableTitle: Probability Optimization Model - Actual Worthless % by Score Range
calibration.v3Desc1: The TA ML Model uses machine learning to analyze 17 different signals about each option and the underlying stock to estimate the probability it will expire worthless. This table shows the historical accuracy of predictions at different probability levels.
calibration.v3Testing: Walk-forward temporal validation on the comprehensive historical database (all Swedish options, not just tracked ones). The model trains on historical data, then predicts on future periods it never saw. The 70.06% actual worthless rate at 70-80% represents realistic expectation for new options the model hasn't encountered. Sample size of 1,617,173 at 70-80% provides extremely tight confidence intervals (±0.07 pp), proving the model generalizes well beyond training data.
calibration.v3DataRequirement: TA Model uses 17 standard technical analysis features (RSI, MACD, ADX, Bollinger Bands, Greeks, etc.) calculated from standard daily price data. The model trains on daily option snapshots—one record per option contract per trading day, so the same contract appears multiple times over its lifetime. Of 8,757,990 option-day records (April 2024 - January 2026), 7,375,016 were validated as out-of-sample across 5 temporal folds. No special probability tracking required—just standard market data every stock has.
calibration.v3TableTitle: TA ML Model - Actual Worthless % by Predicted Range

lossAnalysis.title: Loss Analysis - In-the-Money Scenarios
lossAnalysis.subtitle: Historical loss data when options expire in-the-money (ITM). Probability Optimization Model: 263,723 ITM options analyzed. TA ML Model: 2.3M+ ITM options (walk-forward validation).
lossAnalysis.dataSourceV21: Data source: v2_1_loss_analysis_summary_cleaned.csv (263,723 ITM options). Dataset cleaned by removing 2,957 Embracer Group AB options with artificial losses from stock split event.
lossAnalysis.dataSourceV3: Data source: ta_v3_calibration_results.csv (walk-forward validation on 2.3M+ ITM options).
lossAnalysis.distributionSummaryTitle: Loss Distribution Summary (70-80% Primary Operating Zone)

agreementAnalysis.title: Combined Analysis
agreementAnalysis.disagreeDescV21Higher: Probability Optimization Model ({{v21Score}}) is bullish, but TA ML Model ({{taScore}}) is bearish. Difference: {{diff}} points.
agreementAnalysis.disagreeDescTAHigher: TA ML Model ({{taScore}}) is bullish, but Probability Optimization Model ({{v21Score}}) is bearish. Difference: {{diff}} points.
agreementAnalysis.disagreeRecommendation: Use with caution. Mixed signals suggest elevated uncertainty. Consider additional confirmation.
agreementAnalysis.agreeDescStrong: Both models strongly bullish. Probability Optimization: {{v21Score}}, TA ML: {{taScore}}. Difference: {{diff}} points.
agreementAnalysis.agreeDescModerate: Both models aligned with moderate conviction. Probability Optimization: {{v21Score}}, TA ML: {{taScore}}. Difference: {{diff}} points.
agreementAnalysis.agreeRecommendationStrong: High confidence signal. Both models strongly support this position.
agreementAnalysis.agreeRecommendationModerate: Moderate confidence signal. Both models support, but with some variance.

keyInsights.title: 💡 Key Insights: The Premium Collection Sweet Spot
keyInsights.twoModelsText: When predicting 70-80% probability, Probability Optimization Model achieved 83.8% accuracy (options actually expired worthless), TA ML Model achieved 70.06%. Different market coverage explains the difference: Probability Optimization Model specializes on deeply-tracked options (19.8K sample); TA Model validates on comprehensive historical data (1.6M sample across all market conditions).
keyInsights.dataMetricsText: Actual Worthless % varies by model and prediction range. Review the detailed tables below for comprehensive performance data.
keyInsights.statisticalPrecisionText: Large sample sizes (1.6M for TA ML Model, 19.8K for Probability Model at 70-80%) provide high confidence in the estimates. Confidence intervals show the range where true Actual Worthless % likely falls.
keyInsights.riskAssessmentText: Lower Actual Worthless % indicates higher risk. Evaluate model performance across prediction ranges to support your trading decisions.
keyInsights.source: Source: TA ML Model validated on 7.4M+ out-of-sample records (April 2024 - January 2026, May 2026 retrain). Probability Optimization Model on 72K+ tracked options.

temporalStability.title: Temporal Stability Analysis (Per-Fold 70-80% Bucket)
temporalStability.description: This analysis shows how the TA ML Model's Actual Worthless % at the 70-80% prediction range varied across different market periods. Each "fold" represents a distinct testing window in walk-forward validation. The May 2026 retrain achieves 70.06% overall at the 70-80% bucket. Individual periods show deviation from this baseline due to market regime changes.
temporalStability.obs1Text: Variation ranges from -0.7pp (Fold 2) to +15.1pp (Fold 4), showing how market regimes affect calibration
temporalStability.obs2Text: Dec 2024 - Mar 2025 saw the lowest performance (69.4% actual vs 70.1% baseline). This reflects a temporary market regime shift during this period.
temporalStability.obs3Text: Folds 4-5 (Jun 2025 - Jan 2026) show above-baseline performance (76.5-85.2%), suggesting the model is well-calibrated for recent market conditions.
temporalStability.obs4Text: The May 2026 retrain achieved 70.06% Actual Worthless % at 70-80% predictions in aggregate across 7.4M out-of-sample records. Period-to-period variation is visible in the fold-level data above.

v21Breakdown.title: Probability Optimization Model
v21Breakdown.historicalPeakNote: Highest probability this option has shown historically
v21Breakdown.supportStrengthNote: Strength of support level around strike price
v21Breakdown.interp90: Very strong put option. High probability of expiration worthless. Excellent entry point.
v21Breakdown.interp80: Strong put option. Good probability of expiration worthless. Solid opportunity.
v21Breakdown.interp70: Moderate put option. Reasonable probability of expiration worthless. Acceptable risk/reward.
v21Breakdown.interp60: Weak put option. Lower probability of expiration worthless. Marginal opportunity.
v21Breakdown.interp0: Very weak put option. Low probability of expiration worthless. Avoid.

portfolioGenerator.title: Automatic Portfolio Generator
portfolioGenerator.powLearnTitle: Learn what PoW means and about the probability methods
portfolioGenerator.powWhatIsDesc: PoW = Probability of Worthless — The probability that an option will expire worthless (meaning you keep the premium).
portfolioGenerator.descriptionText: The Portfolio Generator evaluates each option and ranks them based on your selected optimization strategy, then automatically selects a diversified set (one option per stock). Maximize Returns prioritizes highest risk-adjusted returns. Minimize Capital prioritizes lowest capital requirements while maintaining quality. Balanced optimizes for both return and capital efficiency. Scored Models ranks options using a weighted blend of the Probability Optimization Model and TA ML Model scores.
portfolioGenerator.maxCapitalDesc: Limits total underlying value across all options
portfolioGenerator.strategyCapitalDesc: Prioritize lowest capital requirements while maintaining quality
portfolioGenerator.strategyScoredDesc: Rank by Probability Optimization Model + TA ML model scores
portfolioGenerator.totalPremium: Total Premium: {{value}} SEK (Based on {{underlying}} SEK underlying value, {{cost}} SEK transaction cost per option included)
portfolioGenerator.totalRiskOfLoss: Total Calculated Risk of Loss: {{value}} SEK
portfolioGenerator.toastGeneratingDesc: Analyzing options and building your portfolio...

optionsTable.filterHint: Click on column headers to filter
optionsTable.powBtnTitle: Learn what PoW means and about the probability methods
optionsTable.powDesc: PoW = Probability of Worthless — The probability that an option will expire worthless (meaning you keep the premium).

columnManager.title: Manage Table Columns
columnManager.description: Drag to reorder columns and check/uncheck to show/hide them. Click Save to persist your preferences.

portfolioColumnManager.title: Manage Portfolio Table Columns
portfolioColumnManager.description: Drag to reorder columns and check/uncheck to show/hide them. These settings are independent from the main options table. Click Save to persist your preferences.

portfolioOptionsTable.potentialLossDesc1: This field represents the maximum potential loss if the stock price falls to its IV-based lower bound at expiration.
portfolioOptionsTable.potentialLossDesc2: The lower bound is derived from implied volatility analysis to estimate worst-case scenarios for the option position.
